description_long: This is a compact, single-story tobacco shop with a flat, slightly weathered roof and a rectangular footprint. The facade features a wide, metallic rolling shutter covering the main entrance and windows, suggesting the shop is currently closed. Above the shutter, a prominent cream and yellow sign reads 'Bistrot Bar Tabac Presse' with a red 'ELFORT' logo in the center, indicating the shop's function as a bar, tobacco, and press outlet. On either side of the entrance, there are glass display cases or poster frames, each containing advertising posters or product information. The exterior is primarily constructed from metal and concrete, with some glass elements in the display areas. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, typical of small urban commercial buildings in France.
description: A small, single-story urban tobacco shop with a flat roof, metal rolling shutter, and prominent cream and yellow signage reading 'Bistrot Bar Tabac Presse'. The facade includes glass display cases with posters and a central entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full signage, central entrance with a metal shutter, and both side display cases with posters.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated front view, still showing the signage and entrance, but the left side display case is more visible.
description_view_2: Similar to view 1, with the right side display case more visible and the signage still prominent.
description_view_3: Frontal view with minimal perspective, showing the signage, entrance, and both display cases symmetrically.
attribute_storefront_signage: Large cream and yellow sign with black and red text reading 'Bistrot Bar Tabac Presse' and a central red 'ELFORT' logo, made of painted metal or plastic, mounted above the entrance, not illuminated.
attribute_window_displays: Two glass display cases on either side of the entrance, containing posters or product advertisements, arranged vertically with visible frames.
attribute_advertising_posters: Advertising posters are present in the side display cases, featuring colorful graphics and text, likely promoting tobacco products, magazines, or local events. Posters are medium-sized and protected by glass.
attribute_entrance_type: Single central entrance with a wide metal rolling shutter, likely concealing a glass or wooden door behind it. No handles or transparency visible due to the shutter being closed.
attribute_architectural_style: Contemporary utilitarian style, characterized by a flat roof, simple rectangular form, metal shutters, and minimal ornamentation, typical of small urban commercial buildings in France.
